content.problemStatement:
Let's define a function F(X)F(X)F(X) as follows:

F(X)=XYF(X) = \frac{X}{Y}F(X)=YX‚Äč

where YYY is the largest perfect square that divides XXX.

For example,


The largest perfect square that divides 121212 is 444. Hence F(12)=124=3F(12) =  \frac{12}{4} = 3F(12)=412‚Äč=3.
The largest perfect square that divides 363636 is 363636. Hence F(36)=3636=1F(36) =  \frac{36}{36} = 1F(36)=3636‚Äč=1.
The largest perfect square that divides 777 is 111. Hence F(7)=71=7F(7) =  \frac{7}{1} = 7F(7)=17‚Äč=7.
You are given an array AAA consisting of NNN integers. A pair of integer (iii, jjj) is called Good if 1‚Č§i<j‚Č§N1 \leq i \lt j \leq N1‚Č§i<j‚Č§N and  F(Ai‚ąóAj)>1F(A_i * A_j) \gt 1F(Ai‚Äč‚ąóAj‚Äč)>1. Find the number of Good pairs.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN.
The second line of each testcase contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots , A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer - the number of Good pairs.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106.
